# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata: 
   name: my-app # or whatever your app's Name in k8s configmap and secret is named like that, it should be unique within the namespace. for example "mycluster" can not exist simultaneously as an AWS cluster & container instance at same time due to potential conflicts between different resources on this environment
   labels: 
     app: my-app # or whatever your app's Name in k8s configmap and secret is named like that, it should be unique within the namespace. for example "mycluster" can not exist simultaneously as an AWS cluster & container instance at same time due to potential conflicts between different resources on this environment
spec: 
   replicas: 3 # Number of pod copies you want in your deployment - must match desiredState configuration state if specified and template is used. Defaults the number of containers based off Dockerfile instructions, but can be overwritten by using --dry-run mode for testing purposes (e.g., `--replicas=1`)
   selector:  # The labels to select Pod(s)/Pod Template/Controller that this Deployment replicates across must match these Labels in the pod specification, otherwise it is omitted from updates or deletions - Must be specified if at least one of Selector and InitContainer matching criteria are provided. If both a `configMap` key-value pair matches AND selector NOT found with MatchLabels/MatchExpressions then this template instance will not have any instantiations
     matchLabels: # Set label(key, value) pairs that must exist for select pod(containers), or only on Pod if specified. If empty Selector is selected without a key and no labels should be watched by the controller, as all known instances of this template (including uninitialized ones at least once during scaling operations when replacing controlled resources using declarative configs) are deleted after their current pod label(key, value). 
       app: my-app # or whatever your application's Name in k8s secret and namespace is named like that. It should be unique within the Kubernetes cluster - for example "mycluster" can not exist simultaneously as an AWS EKS Cluster & Container Instance at same time due to potential conflicts between different resources on this environment
   template:  # Dockerfile instructions are used and then run inside a pod (i.e., instantiated by kubelet/dockerd for each container in the Pod), but you can also use ConfigMaps, Secrets or built-in volume if they're suitable - You need to make sure that this template is idempotent as changes on an already running Kubernetes pod will not cause a new instantiation of your app into existence.
     metadata: 
       labels: # Set label(key, value) pairs for the Pod Template Instantiated by kubelet/dockerd - it must match selectors from .selector in same ReplicaSet and its corresponding Containers are allowed to access this resource (even if there is no specified Labels), otherwise they will not be updated or deleted.
         app: my-app # set the label that matches your application's Name, so kubelet/dockerd can find it on running pod(containes). It should match 'mycluster', for example "mycontainer" as well and in all resources named like this within same namespace - to prevent conflicts.
     spec: 
       containers: # A collection of container instances (i.e., Dockerfile instructions) that make up the Pod/Container(s). Minimum one should be specified, more than necessary if you have multiple builders or consumers in your application for different purposes like building images and pushing to repositories etc.. The name must match exactly with a Container Name set here; otherwise it is omitted from updates. 
         - # If Dockerfile exists at location/path: container_name (optional, can be skipped), you need not provide this if the image or app's repository supports default tag(tag). It could look like '- name=my-app', which tells k8s to use existing build artifact from 'DockerFile'/location where your Dockerfile is located and copy all content of it into a single docker container.
           # For my case, we only have one app so its image would be provided as -> spec: template/spec > containers >> name=my-app in below line 170 on the code snippet above but for other apps you need to provide build_artifacts by defining them under 'buildArtifact' key.
           # It should look like this example : imagePullSecrets -> myRegistryCredentialName, name: "my-app" (or whatever your app is named in k8S secret and namespace) , pullPolicy:(If not specified Dockerfile will use the default policy ie if found it uses else Policies/Deployments are allowed to Pull image from Registry only with authentication), dockerFile: "path_to_DockerFle", args: ["arg1","arg2"], env : [{name:"VARNAME" , value:"value"}], livenessProbe, readinessProbe
